response.justification: This candidate is a full-stack web and systems software engineer with roughly 14 years of experience across Node.js, TypeScript, React, and D-lang. While there is some low-latency and performance-adjacent work at Dunnhumby, the profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role: there is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator optimization, deep learning workload analysis, or any exposure to GPU, SIMD, or MIMD architectures. The educational background (BS in CS) meets the tenure threshold on paper, but the accumulated experience is entirely in web, mobile, and general backend domains rather than the hardware and AI infrastructure engineering this position demands.
strengths: ~14 years of continuous software engineering experience demonstrating sustained technical growth
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby using async I/O and performance-oriented databases
Some embedded and hardware-adjacent exposure (robotic arm development, C/C++ at National Instruments)
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No AI/ML or deep learning workload expertise of any kind; AI work is limited to MCP tooling and agentic dev workflows
No GPU architecture, SIMD, MIMD, or hardware-software co-design background — all preferred qualifications are absent
